Container.Name: SimpleLogin-Postfix
Container.Overview:
SimpleLogin Postfix is the Mail Transfer Agent (MTA) component of the self-hosted SimpleLogin stack.
WHAT THIS DOES: This container is responsible for receiving inbound emails from the internet and routing them to the SimpleLogin-AIO application, as well as sending outbound emails generated by your aliases back to the internet.
MAIL FLOW: 1. Internet sends an email to shopping@yourdomain.com. 2. The email arrives at this Postfix container on Port 25. 3. Postfix queries the SimpleLogin PostgreSQL database to verify the alias exists. 4. Postfix routes the mail internally to the SimpleLogin-AIO container. 5. SimpleLogin-AIO processes the email and hands it back to Postfix to send to your real inbox.
THE ISP PORT 25 PROBLEM: Most residential ISPs block outbound traffic on Port 25 to prevent botnets from sending spam. If your ISP blocks Port 25, you cannot use the "direct" delivery method. Instead, you must use an SMTP Relay (SmartHost).
This template has been pre-configured with multiple Relay Modes to bypass your ISP's block: - ProtonMail: Use if you have a ProtonMail Bridge / SMTP token. - Brevo (Sendinblue): Excellent free tier (300 emails/day) for self-hosters. - Gmail: Route through a Gmail account using an App Password. - Mailgun: For higher volume / API driven routing. - Custom: Bring your own SMTP provider.
Select your Relay Mode in the dropdown below, and fill in the corresponding credential variables in the Advanced settings.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: email, postfix, mta, relay, smtp, brevo, mailgun, protonmail, gmail
Container.Changes: [2026-03-21] - Rebuilt SMTP Relay variables to include discrete fields for ProtonMail, Brevo, Gmail, Mailgun, and Custom configurations.

Config.Name: SMTP Inbound Port
Config.Description: Public SMTP port. You MUST forward Port 25 from your router to this Unraid server's IP address so the internet can deliver mail to your aliases. Note: Some ISPs block inbound port 25 as well, requiring a VPS proxy.

Config.Name: SimpleLogin Domain
Config.Description: Your primary alias domain. This must match the EMAIL_DOMAIN variable set in the SimpleLogin-AIO container.

Config.Name: Postfix Hostname
Config.Description: The FQDN (Fully Qualified Domain Name) hostname of this mail server. Typically mail.yourdomain.com. Used in the HELO/EHLO handshake.

Config.Name: Database URI
Config.Description: Connection string for the PostgreSQL database. Postfix connects directly to the database to validate aliases instantly. Must exactly match the AIO container DB_URI.

Config.Name: SMTP Relay Mode
Config.Description: Select how Postfix should route outbound mail. If your ISP blocks Port 25, select a relay provider here and fill in its credentials below.

Config.Name: ProtonMail SMTP Token
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'protonmail', enter your generated SMTP token here. Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Brevo Username
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'brevo', enter your Brevo SMTP login email. Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Brevo Password
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'brevo', enter your Brevo SMTP key/password. Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Gmail Username
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'gmail', enter your full Gmail address (e.g. user@gmail.com). Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Gmail App Password
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'gmail', enter your 16-character Google App Password (do not use your main account password). Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Mailgun Username
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'mailgun', enter your Mailgun SMTP username (e.g. postmaster@yourdomain.com). Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Mailgun Password
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'mailgun', enter your Mailgun SMTP password. Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Custom Relay Host
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'custom', enter the SMTP server host and port (e.g. [smtp.provider.com]:587). Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Custom Relay Username
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'custom', enter the SMTP authentication username. Keep blank otherwise.

Config.Name: Custom Relay Password
Config.Description: If Relay Mode is 'custom', enter the SMTP authentication password. Keep blank otherwise.
